title: Improving alignment of dialogue agents via targeted human judgements
hook: What if chatbots could follow rules and explain their answers with real evidence?
promise: You will see how dialogue agents can be trained to follow rules and cite evidence using human feedback.
prerequisite_note: Bring curiosity about how AI can be taught to behave safely and give reliable answers.

glossary.plain: Making sure an AI does what humans want and avoids harmful behaviors.
A chatbot or AI that interacts with people through conversation.
Explicit guidelines that the AI must follow during its responses.
The agent provides sources or reasons for its answers, not just opinions.
People judge the AI's answers to help improve its behavior.
A training method where AI learns from human preferences.

cards.title: Teaching Chatbots to Follow Rules
body: Discover how Sparrow learned to obey rules and cite evidence using human guidance.

cards.title: Why chatbots need rules
body: Imagine a chatbot that sometimes makes things up, gives unsafe advice, or cannot explain itself. This is a real problem for AI assistants.
Sparrow was built to address these issues by training a chatbot to follow explicit rules and back up its answers with evidence.

cards.title: How Sparrow learns to align
body: First, humans set rules for the chatbot and collect many example responses. Then, human judges rate which answers follow the rules and provide good evidence.
The AI uses these ratings to learn which behaviors are rewarded, gradually improving its ability to obey rules and cite sources.
cards.middle: AI gets human feedback on responses
cards.caption: Sparrow's learning loop connects rules, human judgement, and improved chatbot behavior.

cards.title: What changes with human-judged rules?
body: See how a chatbot's answers differ before and after it learns from human-judged rules.
cards.offNote: Chatbot may give unsafe or unsupported answers, and cannot explain its reasoning.
cards.onNote: Chatbot follows rules, avoids unsafe topics, and cites evidence for its answers.

cards.title: Learning from targeted human feedback
body: Sparrow's core insight is to use explicit rules and targeted human judgement to shape a chatbot's behavior.
Instead of just predicting language, Sparrow is trained to satisfy human judges who care about safety and evidence.

cards.title: How strict should the rules be?
body: Adjust how tightly the chatbot follows rules versus how freely it can answer.
cards.low: The chatbot is creative but may make mistakes or unsafe claims.
cards.mid: The chatbot balances creativity with some rule-following and evidence.
cards.high: The chatbot is very safe but may sound less natural or flexible.

cards.title: Which parts make a safer chatbot?
body: Pick which ingredients you think are crucial for training a rule-following, evidence-citing dialogue agent.

choices.label: Explicit rules
choices.note: Define what the chatbot is allowed to say.

choices.label: Human feedback
choices.note: Humans judge which answers are good or bad.

choices.label: Evidence citation
choices.note: Require the agent to back up its claims.

choices.label: Dialogue dataset
choices.note: Provide many example conversations to learn from.

cards.title: Sparrow's safer, more reliable answers
body: Sparrow was much better at following rules and providing evidence compared to previous chatbots.
Human judges preferred Sparrow's responses and found them safer in real conversations.
cards.caption: Sparrow showed big improvements in rule-following, evidence, and safety.

cards.title: Why Sparrow changed the field
body: Sparrow proved that chatbots can be taught not just to talk, but to follow rules and explain themselves.
This approach inspired new generations of safer, more trustworthy dialogue agents.

recap.label: Set
recap.note: Define rules for safe chatbot behavior

recap.label: Judge
recap.note: Humans rate answers for rule-following and evidence

recap.label: Train
recap.note: AI learns from human feedback to improve

cards.title: Improving alignment of dialogue agents via targeted human judgements
body: Read the source to see how Sparrow's training process was designed and measured.
